description: Key-revocation context golden vector (RFC-ACDP-0014 §4–§5). A producer-signed key-revocation body: the producer (did:web:agents.example.com:test-producer) revokes K1 — the sig-001 test key, fingerprint as pinned by rot-001/fp-001 — signing with its current key K2 (the sig-003 test seed, matching rot-001's K2). Executed arithmetically by scripts/conformance-runner.py exactly like sig-001: JCS canonical form, content_hash, Ed25519 signature over the ASCII content_hash string. Test keypair MUST NOT be used in production.

test_keypair.warning: TEST ONLY. The private seed is publicly known (the sig-003 test seed, reused as rot-001's K2). Never use this keypair for real publications.
test_keypair.role: K2 — the producer's CURRENT key (in verificationMethod AND assertionMethod, per the rot-001 DID document), signing the revocation of K1.

revoked_key.role: K1 — the sig-001 test key, retained in verificationMethod only (rot-001). RFC-ACDP-0010 §6 fingerprint over the raw 32-byte Ed25519 key.

vectors.name: Producer-signed key-revocation context, signed by the current key K2

producer_content.title: Key revocation — key-1 compromised
producer_content.summary: Revocation of the Ed25519 key did:web:agents.example.com:test-producer#key-1, compromised since 2026-05-01T00:00:00.000Z.

metadata.reason: laptop theft; private key material presumed exfiltrated

expected.canonical_form: {"acdp_version":"0.3.0","agent_id":"did:web:agents.example.com:test-producer","contributors":[],"data_refs":[],"derived_from":[],"metadata":{"compromised_since":"2026-05-01T00:00:00.000Z","reason":"laptop theft; private key material presumed exfiltrated","revoked_key_fingerprint":"sha256:139e3940e64b5491722088d9a0d741628fc826e09475d341a780acde3c4b8070"},"summary":"Revocation of the Ed25519 key did:web:agents.example.com:test-producer#key-1, compromised since 2026-05-01T00:00:00.000Z.","supersedes":null,"title":"Key revocation — key-1 compromised","type":"key-revocation","version":1,"visibility":"public"}

publish_request_body.title: Key revocation — key-1 compromised
publish_request_body.summary: Revocation of the Ed25519 key did:web:agents.example.com:test-producer#key-1, compromised since 2026-05-01T00:00:00.000Z.

metadata.reason: laptop theft; private key material presumed exfiltrated

verification_steps: 1. Strip the exclusion set from publish_request_body to produce producer_content; JCS-canonicalize; MUST equal expected.canonical_form.
2. SHA-256 of the canonical bytes with 'sha256:' prefix MUST equal expected.content_hash.
3. Sign the ASCII bytes of expected.content_hash with the K2 test seed; MUST equal expected.signature_value_base64; verification with the K2 public key MUST succeed.

4. RFC-ACDP-0014 §4 shape: type == 'key-revocation'; visibility == 'public'; metadata.revoked_key_fingerprint present in RFC-ACDP-0010 §6 form; metadata.compromised_since is canonical millisecond RFC 3339 UTC. A 0.3.0 registry MUST reject violations with schema_violation.

5. RFC-ACDP-0014 §5 step 2: fingerprint(resolved signing key K2) = sha256:3097e2de… != metadata.revoked_key_fingerprint = sha256:139e3940… (K1). A revocation signed by the revoked key itself MUST be rejected by 0.3.0 registries with key_not_authorized and treated as unverified by consumers.

notes: The revoked fingerprint equals the fp-001 Ed25519 vector and the fingerprint attested by the rcpt-001 receipt — the same K1 story as rot-001. rev-002 builds the consumer-side before/after boundary on this vector.
